I think one of the things I like to do is one of the reasons I'm not as a fan of this one is I like to do that speaking with adjacency, meaning telling a story about one thing in order to tell a story about another thing. I think that works especially well when the thing we're trying to tell people about is something that we're unfamiliar with. So if we're trying to describe a new use case for AI in relationship to your refrigerator, that's going to be tricky. And so I might want to speak with adjacency. I might want to say nobody understands what the hell this is. So let us tell a story that metaphorically applies to our AI refrigerator combo. With a lawn mower, I just feel like everyone knows it already. So I don't need to leave the lawn mowing world for people to make sense of it.
